title: selaginella poperangensis
intro: Selaginella poperangensis, also known as the 'Buddha's hand fern', is a type of spore-producing vascular plant belonging to the family Selaginellaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help to combat various infections and diseases. The health benefits of Selaginella poperangensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. In traditional medicine, Selaginella poperangensis is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
